signals.headline: Core CPI (excluding volatile food and energy) stood at 2
signals.raw: Core CPI (excluding volatile food and energy) stood at 2.6% year-over-year**, slightly better than expected.[1][5] However, this persistent core inflation above 2% is likely to **make the Fed cautious about further interest rate cuts**, at least until policymakers can assess whether wartime price increases prove temporary.[5]

signals.headline: Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Validators Approved Alpenglow Upgrade Last Week | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The upgrade, approved recently, cuts transaction finality from 12 seconds to under 200 milliseconds, positioning Solana for high-frequency trading and real-time payments, potentially driving price targets like $110 by late April.[1][3]

signals.headline: Ethereum Transactions Jumped 41% in Past Seven Days | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Transactions Jumped 41% in Past Seven Days | YES | MED confidence**  
ETH reclaimed activity leadership from Solana, fueled by L2 fee rebates on Base/Arbitrum and tokenized treasury issuance, with price up 8.6% to $2,377 amid ETF AUM of $48B.[1]

signals.headline: Headline CPI rose 3
signals.raw: Headline CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year in March 2026**, the largest annual increase in nearly two years, beating economist expectations of 3.4%.[1][5] On a monthly basis, prices climbed 0.9%.[1] This surge was primarily driven by a **45% spike in oil prices** (from $71 to $103 per barrel between February and March) following the start of the Iran war, which pushed energy prices up 10.9%.[3]

signals.headline: Market implications:** Financial market-implied expectations for 2026 headline CPI inflation rose 0
signals.raw:
Market implications:** Financial market-implied expectations for 2026 headline CPI inflation rose 0.8 percentage points to 3.3% following the Iran war.[3] Goldman Sachs has raised its December 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast by a full percentage point since the conflict began.[3]

The search results do not contain any announcements from today (April 22, 2026) regarding inflation.
